title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a message from a library assistant.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Hello Tom, this is the Central Library. The travel guide you reserved has arrived, and it is waiting for you at the front desk. We normally keep reserved books for seven days. ||Woman: [1]However, another reader has requested this title too, so please collect it by Saturday afternoon or it will be offered to them instead[/1]. Remember to bring your library card. Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hello Tom, this is the Central Library. The travel guide you reserved has arrived, and it is waiting for you at the front desk. We normally keep reserved books for seven days.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: However, another reader has requested this title too, so please collect it by Saturday afternoon or it will be offered to them instead. Remember to bring your library card. Thank you.

questions.question: What does the library assistant tell Tom?

options.A: He must return a book by Saturday.
options.B: He should collect a book before the weekend ends.
options.C: He needs to reserve a different guidebook.

questions.explanation: Tom is asked to collect the reserved travel guide by Saturday afternoon (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about an art course.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you enjoy the evening drawing course? I’ve been thinking of joining next term. ||Man: Yes, though I expected it to be more advanced. We spent the first lesson drawing simple objects. ||Woman: Was that disappointing? ||Man: Not really. [2]There were only six students, so the teacher had enough time to give each of us useful individual advice[/2]. That mattered more than the level.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy the evening drawing course? I’ve been thinking of joining next term.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, though I expected it to be more advanced. We spent the first lesson drawing simple objects.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was that disappointing?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not really. There were only six students, so the teacher had enough time to give each of us useful individual advice. That mattered more than the level.

questions.question: What did the man value about the course?
questions.explanation: He appreciated that a small class meant the teacher could give individual advice (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement at a sports centre.
extracts.audio_script: Guide: Could swimmers please note a timetable change for this afternoon. The children's lesson will take place as usual in the teaching pool at four o’clock. ||Guide: [3]The main pool will be closed between three and five for a school competition, so adults wanting to swim lengths should come after five o’clock today[/3]. The gym and café are unaffected.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: Could swimmers please note a timetable change for this afternoon. The children's lesson will take place as usual in the teaching pool at four o’clock.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: The main pool will be closed between three and five for a school competition, so adults wanting to swim lengths should come after five o’clock today. The gym and café are unaffected.

questions.question: What should adults who want to swim do?
questions.explanation: The main pool is closed until five, so adult swimmers are advised to come later (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a dog walker.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Helen, you left an office job to become a professional dog walker. Was that because you wanted more exercise? ||Woman: That was a benefit, and I’ve always loved dogs. But [4]the main reason was that I needed work I could organise around collecting my own children from school each afternoon[/4]. It’s busy, but I can choose my hours much more easily now.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Helen, you left an office job to become a professional dog walker. Was that because you wanted more exercise?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That was a benefit, and I’ve always loved dogs. But the main reason was that I needed work I could organise around collecting my own children from school each afternoon. It’s busy, but I can choose my hours much more easily now.

questions.question: Why did Helen become a dog walker?

options.A: to choose working times that suited her family
options.B: to spend more time exercising outdoors
options.C: to train for a job caring for animals

questions.explanation: Helen says she needed work she could organise around collecting her children (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer at a concert ticket office.
extracts.audio_script: Customer: Are there any tickets left for Saturday’s jazz concert? ||Assistant: Only expensive seats for the evening show, I’m afraid. There are cheaper seats for the afternoon performance, but it starts at two. ||Customer: That’s fine. [5]I’d rather go in the afternoon and pay less, as I’m mainly interested in hearing the music, not having the best view[/5]. ||Assistant: I’ll book one for you.

dialogue.speaker: customer
dialogue.text: Are there any tickets left for Saturday’s jazz concert?

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: Only expensive seats for the evening show, I’m afraid. There are cheaper seats for the afternoon performance, but it starts at two.

dialogue.speaker: customer
dialogue.text: That’s fine. I’d rather go in the afternoon and pay less, as I’m mainly interested in hearing the music, not having the best view.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: I’ll book one for you.

questions.question: Which ticket does the customer choose?

options.C: a ticket with the best possible view

questions.explanation: The customer chooses the cheaper afternoon performance rather than paying more (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear a student discussing a work placement with a friend.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you accepted that work placement at the newspaper office? It sounds perfect for your journalism course. ||Woman: I wanted to, but it’s across town and I couldn’t afford the train every day for six weeks. ||Man: That’s a shame. ||Woman: Actually, [6]the organiser emailed this morning to say they’ll pay my travel costs, so I’ve just written back to accept the place[/6]. ||Man: Brilliant!

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you accepted that work placement at the newspaper office? It sounds perfect for your journalism course.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I wanted to, but it’s across town and I couldn’t afford the train every day for six weeks.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That’s a shame.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Actually, the organiser emailed this morning to say they’ll pay my travel costs, so I’ve just written back to accept the place.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Brilliant!

questions.question: Why has the woman decided to accept the placement?

options.B: It has been shortened to a few days.
options.C: It is closer to her home than expected.

questions.explanation: She has accepted because the organiser will cover the cost of her travel (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a tent.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Can I borrow your tent for the music festival next weekend? The weather should be dry, and I’ve already bought a sleeping bag. ||Man: You can, but I noticed the zip on the entrance doesn’t close properly when I used it last month. ||Woman: Oh. ||Man: [7]I’ll take it to the outdoor shop tomorrow and have the zip repaired before I lend it to you[/7]. ||Woman: That would be great.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Can I borrow your tent for the music festival next weekend? The weather should be dry, and I’ve already bought a sleeping bag.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You can, but I noticed the zip on the entrance doesn’t close properly when I used it last month.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ll take it to the outdoor shop tomorrow and have the zip repaired before I lend it to you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That would be great.

questions.question: What will the man do before lending the tent?

options.A: clean it after a previous trip
options.B: get a part of it fixed
options.C: buy a sleeping bag for the woman

questions.explanation: He says he will have the tent's zip repaired before lending it (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio presenter talking about a farm shop.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Green Hill Farm Shop sells vegetables, fruit and bread produced in the local area. It is open every day except Monday, and home deliveries can be arranged through its website. ||Woman: [8]During June, shoppers who bring their own bag will receive ten per cent off fresh fruit and vegetables, as the farm is trying to reduce plastic packaging[/8]. Ask staff for details at the till.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Green Hill Farm Shop sells vegetables, fruit and bread produced in the local area. It is open every day except Monday, and home deliveries can be arranged through its website.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: During June, shoppers who bring their own bag will receive ten per cent off fresh fruit and vegetables, as the farm is trying to reduce plastic packaging. Ask staff for details at the till.

questions.question: What special offer does the farm shop have in June?

options.A: free home delivery for online customers
options.B: lower prices for customers using their own bags
options.C: extra bread with every fruit purchase

questions.explanation: Customers bringing their own bag receive a discount on fruit and vegetables in June (evidence [8]).
